claim_boundary: v6.0.6 freezes an interpretive ontology and a provenance firewall. It does not claim established physics as a BHSM derivation, close the B8/S7-to-Berger-S3 reduction, or change any observable prediction.

rows.action_relation: fiber restriction/integration and consistent truncation unproved
rows.map: i_x:p_H^-1(x)=S3 -> S7 after x in S4 is selected
rows.measure_relation: conditional fiber measure after vertical scale/orientation
rows.metric_relation: round vertical restriction for the standard round submersion; generic Berger squashing requires an unselected compatible S7 metric
rows.operator_relation: vertical operator only; not the full S7 Laplacian

rows.topology: no canonical three-dimensional slice of seven-dimensional S7 selected

rows.action_relation: restriction is not automatically a consistent truncation
rows.operator_relation: ambient and intrinsic Laplacians differ by extrinsic data
rows.topology: a selected Hopf fiber is an embedded S3; other embeddings are nonunique

rows.metric_relation: requires selected four-dimensional orbit and submersion metric
rows.topology: S7/Sp(1)=S4, not S3; another group action would be required

rows.measure_relation: normalization inherited from S7 inner product
rows.metric_relation: effective metric must follow from the projected kinetic form
rows.topology: field-sector reduction rather than a topological identification
S7 is closed, or is the seven-boundary of B8; an S3 sub-boundary is not automatic

rows.action_relation: dimensionally compatible parent product action absent
rows.map: product projection or selected S7 fiber
rows.metric_relation: legacy Berger metric applies directly only on an independently postulated S3
rows.topology: independent S3 is coherent in a product theory but is not itself the S7 reduction
legacy S3 retained on its declared domain

strongest_candidate: selected quaternionic Hopf fiber plus a proved action-consistent invariant-mode truncation
